kitchen.title: Kitchen Renovation
kitchen.description: Complete kitchen remodeling with modern designs, custom cabinetry, and premium countertops.
kitchen.longDescription: Transform your kitchen into the heart of your home with our comprehensive renovation services. From custom cabinetry and premium countertops to state-of-the-art appliances and innovative storage solutions, we create kitchens that are both beautiful and functional.

bathroom.title: Bathroom Renovation
bathroom.description: Transform your bathroom into a spa-like retreat with luxury fixtures and finishes.
bathroom.longDescription: Create your personal sanctuary with our bathroom renovation expertise. We specialize in luxury spa-inspired designs, modern fixtures, custom tile work, and innovative storage solutions that maximize both style and functionality.

wholeHouse.title: Whole House Renovation
wholeHouse.description: Full-scale home transformations from concept to completion.
wholeHouse.longDescription: Experience a complete home transformation with our whole house renovation services. We manage every aspect of your project, from initial design concepts through final completion, ensuring a seamless renovation experience.

basement.title: Basement Remodeling
basement.description: Convert your basement into functional living space. Ranked 3rd in Best of Vancouver.
basement.longDescription: Unlock the full potential of your home with our award-winning basement remodeling services. Whether you want a home theater, gym, guest suite, or home office, we transform underutilized basements into beautiful, functional living spaces.

cabinet.title: Cabinet Refacing
cabinet.description: Refresh your kitchen look with professional cabinet refacing services.
cabinet.longDescription: Give your kitchen a stunning new look without the cost of a full renovation. Our cabinet refacing services include new doors, drawer fronts, and hardware, transforming your kitchen's appearance while preserving your existing cabinet boxes.

commercial.title: Commercial Renovation
commercial.description: Professional commercial space renovations for offices, retail, and restaurants.
commercial.longDescription: Elevate your business space with our commercial renovation expertise. We handle office build-outs, retail store renovations, restaurant redesigns, and more, delivering professional results that enhance your brand and customer experience.

kitchen.q1: How long does a kitchen renovation typically take?
kitchen.a1: A typical kitchen renovation takes 4-8 weeks depending on the scope. Simple updates like cabinet refacing may take 1-2 weeks, while complete gut renovations with custom cabinetry can take 8-12 weeks.
kitchen.q2: What is the average cost of a kitchen renovation in {area}?
kitchen.a2: Kitchen renovations in {area} typically range from $25,000 to $75,000+ depending on size and finishes. We provide free detailed quotes after an in-home consultation.
kitchen.q3: Do I need permits for my kitchen renovation?
kitchen.a3: Permits are required for electrical, plumbing, and structural changes. We handle all permit applications and ensure your renovation meets {area} building codes.

bathroom.q1: How long does a bathroom renovation in {area} take?
bathroom.a1: Most bathroom renovations in {area} take 2-4 weeks. A simple cosmetic update may take 1 week, while a complete renovation with layout changes can take 4-6 weeks.
bathroom.q2: Can I use my bathroom during renovation?
bathroom.a2: Typically no, but we work efficiently to minimize disruption. If you have multiple bathrooms, we can help you plan around the renovation schedule.
bathroom.q3: What waterproofing do you use for bathrooms?
bathroom.a3: We use industry-leading waterproofing systems including Schluter and RedGard to ensure your bathroom is properly protected against moisture damage.

whole-house.q1: Can I live in my home during a whole house renovation in {area}?
whole-house.a1: It depends on the scope. For major renovations in {area}, we often recommend temporary relocation for safety and efficiency. We can help coordinate phased work if staying is preferred.
whole-house.q2: How do you manage a whole house renovation project?
whole-house.a2: We assign a dedicated project manager who coordinates all trades, maintains the schedule, and provides regular updates. You have a single point of contact throughout.
whole-house.q3: Do I need permits for a whole house renovation in {area}?
whole-house.a3: Yes, whole house renovations typically require multiple permits for structural, electrical, and plumbing work. We handle all {area} permit applications and inspections.

basement.q1: Do I need a permit to finish my basement in {area}?
basement.a1: Yes, basement finishing requires building permits in {area}. We handle all permit applications and inspections as part of our service.
basement.q2: How do you handle basement moisture issues?
basement.a2: We assess moisture levels and implement appropriate solutions including drainage improvements, vapor barriers, and proper insulation before finishing.
basement.q3: Can I add a bathroom to my basement?
basement.a3: Yes, we can add bathrooms to basements. If below the sewer line, we install sewage ejector systems. All plumbing meets {area} building codes.

cabinet.q1: What is the difference between refacing and replacing cabinets?
cabinet.a1: Refacing keeps your existing cabinet boxes and replaces doors, drawer fronts, and hardware. It costs 40-50% less than full replacement and takes less time.
cabinet.q2: How long does cabinet refacing take in {area}?
cabinet.a2: Cabinet refacing in {area} typically takes 3-5 days for an average kitchen. Your kitchen remains functional throughout most of the process.
cabinet.a3: Most solid wood and plywood cabinet boxes can be refaced if they are structurally sound. We assess your cabinets during a free in-home consultation in {area}.

commercial.q1: Do you work during business hours?
commercial.a1: We offer flexible scheduling including evenings and weekends to minimize disruption to your business operations. We can phase work to keep parts of your space operational.
commercial.q2: Are you experienced with {area} commercial building codes?
commercial.a2: Yes, our team is experienced with {area} commercial building codes, accessibility requirements, and fire safety regulations. We ensure full compliance with all commercial standards.
commercial.q3: Can you handle tenant improvements in {area}?
commercial.a3: Yes, we specialize in tenant improvements (TI) for offices, retail spaces, and restaurants in {area}. We work with landlords and property managers on approval processes.

homeFaq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
homeFaq.subtitle: Common questions about our renovation services

showroomPage.heroSubtitle: Experience renovation materials, designs, and finishes firsthand
showroomPage.ctaDescription: Book a free consultation at our showroom. Our design team will help you explore options and plan your renovation.
showroomPage.faq1q: Do I need an appointment to visit the showroom?
showroomPage.faq1a: Walk-ins are welcome, but we recommend booking an appointment so our design team can dedicate time to discuss your project in detail.
showroomPage.faq2q: What can I see at the Reno Stars showroom?
showroomPage.faq2a: Our showroom features material samples, cabinet displays, countertop options, tile selections, and design inspiration for kitchen, bathroom, and whole house renovations.
showroomPage.faq3q: Is there parking available at the showroom?
showroomPage.faq3a: Yes, free parking is available directly in front of our showroom location in Richmond.
showroomPage.faq4q: Can I bring my renovation plans to the showroom?
showroomPage.faq4a: Absolutely! Bring your floor plans, photos, or inspiration images. Our team can review them and provide professional recommendations during your visit.

projectsFaqs.q1: How do I get started with a renovation project?
projectsFaqs.a1: Start with a free consultation — contact us online or visit our showroom. We'll discuss your vision, assess the space, and provide a detailed quote and timeline.
projectsFaqs.q2: Can I see examples of your past work?
projectsFaqs.a2: Yes! Browse our portfolio above to see before-and-after photos of completed kitchen, bathroom, and whole house renovation projects across Metro Vancouver.
projectsFaqs.q3: Do you handle permits and inspections?
projectsFaqs.a3: Yes, we manage all necessary permits and coordinate inspections as part of our full-service renovation process, so you don't have to worry about any of the paperwork.

designFaqs.q1: What does the design consultation include?
designFaqs.a1: Our design consultation includes a discussion of your vision, space assessment, material recommendations, 3D visualization when applicable, and a detailed project plan with timeline and budget estimate.
designFaqs.q2: Do you offer 3D renderings of renovation designs?
designFaqs.a2: Yes, we provide 3D visualizations for many projects so you can see exactly how your renovated space will look before any work begins.
designFaqs.q3: How much does a design consultation cost?
designFaqs.a3: Our initial design consultations are free. We believe in helping you explore your options before making any commitment.

step1.title: Initial Consultation
step1.subtitle: Discovery
points: Initial contact via phone, email, Whatsapp, WeChat, or SMS
Confirm renovation areas: kitchen, living room, bathroom, or whole house
Style preference confirmation
Material budget and positioning
Understand renovation needs and expectations
Obtain floor plans and site photos (if available)
step1.faqAnswer: We begin with an initial consultation where you can reach us by phone, email, WhatsApp, WeChat, or SMS. We'll discuss your renovation needs, style preferences, budget, and gather any existing floor plans or photos.

step2.title: Tailored Assessment & Quote
step2.subtitle: On-Site Measurement (if needed) & Transparent Pricing
points: Professional on-site measurement for precise dimensions (if needed)
A comprehensive design proposal illustrating your future space
Clearly defined scope of work, project timeline, permit requirements, and material specifications
A clear and transparent quotation aligned with the proposed design and scope, ensuring full clarity and predictability throughout the process
step2.faqAnswer: We provide professional on-site measurement when needed and create a comprehensive design proposal for your space. You'll receive a clearly defined scope of work with timeline, permit requirements, and a transparent quotation aligned with the proposed design.

step3.title: Confirmation & Contract
step3.subtitle: Finalizing Details & Project Initiation
points: Invite clients to showroom for in-person consultation
Address questions and optimize design details
Confirm design proposal and material samples
Finalize the payment schedule and assist with delivery scheduling
Sign construction contract and collect deposit
Secure the project start date
Establish project team: Project Manager, Construction Manager, Coordinator
step3.faqAnswer: We invite you to our showroom to review material samples and finalize design details. After confirming the payment schedule and signing the contract, we secure your project start date and establish a dedicated project team including a Project Manager, Construction Manager, and Coordinator.

step4.title: Execution & Management
step4.subtitle: Fully Coordinated & Easy Communication
points: A unified project team managing all aspects of your project
No direct interaction with workers needed
Coordinated construction progress to ensure efficiency and consistency
Assistance with delivery scheduling and on-site coordination
Consistent quality control throughout the entire construction process
Fast response and efficient resolution of any issues
step4.faqAnswer: A unified project team manages all aspects of your project. You won't need to interact with workers directly—we coordinate construction progress, delivery scheduling, and maintain consistent quality control throughout. Any issues are resolved quickly and efficiently.

step5.title: Walkthrough & Aftercare
step5.subtitle: Final Checks & Lasting Support
points: Final walkthrough to confirm all work meets expectations
New home handover
Ongoing support for maintenance during warranty period
step5.faqAnswer: We conduct a final walkthrough with you to confirm all work meets your expectations, then hand over your new home. We provide ongoing support for maintenance during the warranty period.

hero.title: Kitchen Renovation Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: Real pricing from completed kitchen renovations across Greater Vancouver. Every number below comes from actual projects — not industry estimates.

tiers.title: Kitchen Renovation Cost by Tier
tiers.subtitle: Based on our completed projects across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Budget-Friendly
budget.description: Cabinet refacing or stock cabinets, laminate countertops, basic backsplash, and standard fixtures. Ideal for condos and townhouses looking for a fresh look without structural changes.

midRange.title: Mid-Range
midRange.description: Custom or semi-custom cabinets, quartz countertops, tile backsplash, updated lighting, and new flooring. The most popular choice for family homes.

highEnd.title: High-End
highEnd.description: Fully custom cabinetry, premium stone countertops, waterfall islands, high-end appliances, structural modifications, and designer finishes. For homeowners who want the best.

factors.title: What Affects Your Kitchen Renovation Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

cabinetry.title: Cabinetry (30–40% of budget)
cabinetry.description: The biggest cost driver. Stock cabinets start around /linear foot, while custom options can exceed /linear foot. Shaker-style white cabinets are the most popular choice in Vancouver.

countertops.title: Countertops (10–15% of budget)
countertops.description: Laminate starts at /sq ft, quartz runs –150/sq ft, and natural stone can exceed /sq ft. Waterfall island edges add –2,000 to the total.

layout.title: Layout Changes
layout.description: Moving plumbing, gas lines, or removing walls significantly increases costs. A layout change can add ,000–15,000 depending on structural requirements and permits.

appliances.title: Appliances
appliances.description: Budget-friendly packages start at ,000–5,000. Mid-range brands like Bosch or KitchenAid run ,000–15,000. Premium brands like Sub-Zero or Wolf can exceed ,000.

plumbing.title: Plumbing & Electrical
plumbing.description: Relocating plumbing or adding circuits adds ,000–8,000. Older Vancouver homes may need electrical panel upgrades to support modern appliances.

permits.title: Permits & Inspections
permits.description: City of Vancouver building permits for kitchen renovations typically cost –1,000. Required when changing plumbing, electrical, gas, or structural elements.

realProjects.title: Real Kitchen Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed kitchen projects with actual budgets and timelines

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips for Your Kitchen Renovation

tip1.title: Keep the Existing Layout
tip1.description: Avoiding plumbing and gas line relocations can save ,000–15,000. Work with your existing kitchen footprint where possible.

tip2.title: Choose Quartz Over Natural Stone
tip2.description: Quartz countertops are more durable, require no sealing, and cost 20–40% less than granite or marble. Plus, they look stunning.

tip3.title: Semi-Custom Cabinets
tip3.description: Semi-custom cabinets offer the look of fully custom at 40–60% of the price. You get to choose door styles, finishes, and configurations.

tip4.title: Plan During Off-Season
tip4.description: Winter months (November–February) are slower for renovators in Vancouver. You may get better pricing and faster timelines by starting then.

cta.heading: Get Your Free Kitchen Renovation Quote
cta.subtitle: Tell us about your project and receive a detailed estimate within 48 hours — no obligation.

faq.q1: How much does a kitchen renovation cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Kitchen renovation costs in Vancouver typically range from $20,000 to $72,000+ based on our completed projects. Budget renovations (cabinet updates, basic countertops) start around $20,000–$27,000, mid-range renovations with custom cabinets and quartz countertops run $28,000–$38,000, and high-end transformations with premium materials can exceed $40,000.
faq.q2: How long does a kitchen renovation take in Vancouver?
faq.a2: Most kitchen renovations in Vancouver take 3 to 6 weeks from demolition to completion. Simple cosmetic updates can be done in 3–4 weeks, while complex renovations involving layout changes, custom cabinetry, or structural work may take 8–10 weeks.
faq.q3: Do I need a permit for a kitchen renovation in Vancouver?
faq.a3: You need a City of Vancouver building permit if your renovation involves changes to plumbing, electrical, gas lines, or structural elements like removing walls. Cosmetic updates like new cabinets, countertops, and backsplash typically do not require permits. Permit costs range from $200 to $1,000.
faq.q4: What is the best countertop material for a kitchen renovation?
faq.a4: Quartz is the most popular countertop choice in our Vancouver kitchen renovations. It offers excellent durability, requires no sealing (unlike granite), resists stains, and comes in a wide variety of colours and patterns. Prices range from $80 to $150 per square foot installed.
faq.q5: Can I renovate my kitchen while living in my home?
faq.a5: Yes, most of our clients continue living in their homes during kitchen renovations. We help set up a temporary kitchen area and schedule work to minimize disruption. Expect to be without a functioning kitchen for 2–4 weeks depending on the scope of work.

hero.title: Bathroom Renovation Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: Real pricing from completed bathroom renovations across Greater Vancouver. Every number comes from actual projects — not industry estimates.

tiers.title: Bathroom Renovation Cost by Tier
tiers.subtitle: Based on our completed projects across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Budget-Friendly
budget.description: Standard tile, basic vanity, updated fixtures, and fresh paint. Ideal for a single bathroom refresh in condos or townhouses without moving plumbing.

midRange.title: Mid-Range
midRange.description: Porcelain or large-format tile, custom vanity, frameless glass shower enclosure, modern fixtures, and heated flooring. The most popular choice for family homes.

highEnd.title: High-End / Multi-Bathroom
highEnd.description: Premium tile, curbless showers, freestanding tubs, custom cabinetry, smart fixtures, and structural modifications. Often includes renovating 2-3+ bathrooms simultaneously.

factors.title: What Affects Your Bathroom Renovation Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

tiles.title: Tile & Flooring (25–35% of budget)
tiles.description: Standard ceramic tile starts at $5/sq ft, porcelain runs $8–15/sq ft, and natural stone can exceed $25/sq ft. Large-format tiles reduce grout lines but cost more to install.

shower.title: Shower / Tub Configuration
shower.description: A tub-to-shower conversion costs $3,000–8,000. Curbless (zero-threshold) showers require custom waterproofing and sloped subfloor, adding $2,000–5,000 vs a standard shower base.

vanity.title: Vanity & Countertop
vanity.description: Stock vanities start at $500–1,500. Semi-custom with quartz top runs $2,000–4,000. Fully custom floating vanities with integrated sinks can exceed $6,000.

plumbing.title: Plumbing Relocation
plumbing.description: Moving drain lines or supply pipes adds $2,000–6,000. Older homes may need new shut-off valves or supply line upgrades. Keeping existing plumbing layout saves significantly.

waterproofing.title: Waterproofing
waterproofing.description: Proper waterproofing with membrane systems (Schluter, Laticrete) costs $1,500–3,500 for a standard shower. Essential in Vancouver's climate to prevent moisture damage behind walls.

permits.title: Permits & Inspections
permits.description: Building permits for bathroom renovations cost $200–800 in Metro Vancouver. Required when moving plumbing, adding new fixtures, or making structural changes.

realProjects.title: Real Bathroom Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed bathroom projects with actual budgets and timelines

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips for Your Bathroom Renovation

tip1.title: Keep Existing Plumbing Layout
tip1.description: Relocating drain lines is one of the biggest cost drivers. Keeping the toilet, shower, and sink in their current positions can save $3,000–6,000.

tip2.title: Choose Large-Format Porcelain Tile
tip2.description: Fewer grout lines mean less labour and a cleaner look. 24x24 or 12x24 porcelain tiles are durable, water-resistant, and more affordable than natural stone.

tip3.title: Renovate Multiple Bathrooms Together
tip3.description: If you need more than one bathroom done, doing them simultaneously saves 10–20% vs separate projects. Contractors can share mobilization costs and bulk-buy materials.

tip4.title: Invest in Waterproofing
tip4.description: Skimping on waterproofing leads to costly repairs later. Proper membrane systems add $1,500–3,500 upfront but prevent $10,000+ in water damage down the road.

cta.heading: Get Your Free Bathroom Renovation Quote
cta.subtitle: Tell us about your project and receive a detailed estimate within 48 hours — no obligation.

faq.q1: How much does a bathroom renovation cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Bathroom renovation costs in Vancouver range from $14,000 to $60,000+ based on our completed projects. A single budget-friendly bathroom starts around $14,000–$20,000, mid-range renovations with custom vanity and glass shower run $20,000–$35,000, and high-end or multi-bathroom projects can exceed $40,000.
faq.q2: How long does a bathroom renovation take?
faq.a2: A single bathroom renovation typically takes 2–5 weeks. Simple cosmetic updates can be done in 2–3 weeks, while projects involving plumbing relocation, curbless showers, or structural changes may take 4–8 weeks. Multi-bathroom projects take 5–11 weeks.
faq.q3: Can I convert my bathtub to a walk-in shower?
faq.a3: Yes, tub-to-shower conversions are one of our most popular requests. The cost ranges from $3,000–$8,000 depending on whether you choose a standard shower base or curbless design. Curbless showers require custom waterproofing and a sloped subfloor.
faq.q4: What is curbless shower and why is it more expensive?
faq.a4: A curbless (zero-threshold) shower has no step or lip at the entry, creating a seamless floor transition. It requires custom waterproofing membrane, a precisely sloped subfloor, and linear drain installation, adding $2,000–$5,000 vs a standard shower base. It is popular for accessibility and modern aesthetics.
faq.q5: Should I renovate multiple bathrooms at the same time?
faq.a5: Yes, renovating multiple bathrooms simultaneously saves 10–20% compared to separate projects. Contractors can share mobilization costs, coordinate trades more efficiently, and buy materials in bulk. We have completed many 2–3 bathroom projects across Greater Vancouver.

hero.title: Whole House Renovation Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: Real pricing from completed whole-home renovations across Greater Vancouver. From condo refreshes to full house transformations — every number comes from actual projects.

tiers.title: Whole House Renovation Cost by Tier
tiers.subtitle: Based on our completed projects across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Cosmetic Refresh
budget.description: Paint throughout, new flooring, updated light fixtures, bathroom refresh (vanity, mirror, hardware), and minor kitchen updates. Ideal for condos and rental properties needing a modern update without structural work.

midRange.title: Comprehensive Renovation
midRange.description: Full kitchen renovation with new cabinets and countertops, bathroom remodels, new flooring throughout, fresh paint, updated electrical and plumbing fixtures, and new lighting. The most popular choice for homeowners planning to stay long-term.

highEnd.title: Premium Transformation
highEnd.description: Complete gut renovation including structural changes, open-concept layouts, high-end kitchen and bathrooms, custom millwork, premium flooring, smart home integration, and designer finishes throughout. For homeowners who want a truly custom home.

factors.title: What Affects Your Whole House Renovation Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

scope.title: Scope & Square Footage
scope.description: The single biggest factor. A 600 sq ft condo refresh costs far less than a 2,500 sq ft house gut renovation. Define your must-haves vs nice-to-haves early to control budget.

kitchen.title: Kitchen (30–40% of total budget)
kitchen.description: Kitchens are typically the most expensive room. Custom cabinets, quartz countertops, and appliance upgrades can run $20,000–$72,000 on their own. See our kitchen cost guide for detailed breakdowns.

bathrooms.title: Bathrooms (15–25% of total budget)
bathrooms.description: Each bathroom renovation adds $14,000–$40,000 depending on size and finishes. Waterproofing, tile work, and fixture quality drive costs. Multi-bathroom homes see significant savings per unit.

flooring.title: Flooring (10–15% of total budget)
flooring.description: Engineered hardwood runs $8–$15/sq ft installed. Luxury vinyl plank (LVP) offers a budget-friendly alternative at $5–$10/sq ft. Tile for wet areas adds $12–$25/sq ft.

structural.title: Structural Changes
structural.description: Removing walls, adding windows, or reconfiguring layouts adds $5,000–$25,000+. Load-bearing wall removal requires engineering reports and permits. Open-concept conversions are popular but costly.

permits.title: Permits & Professional Fees
permits.description: Building permits in Metro Vancouver typically cost $500–$2,500 for whole house renovations. Budget an additional $2,000–$5,000 for architectural drawings, engineering reports, and inspections.

realProjects.title: Real Whole House Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed whole-house projects with actual budgets and timelines

included.title: What's Typically Included in a Whole House Renovation
included.subtitle: A comprehensive renovation touches every room and system

item1.title: Kitchen Renovation
item1.description: New cabinets, countertops, backsplash, sink, faucet, and updated appliance connections. Layout changes available for mid-range and premium tiers.

item2.title: Bathroom Remodels
item2.description: New vanity, toilet, shower or tub, tile work, waterproofing, and updated plumbing fixtures. Each bathroom fully refreshed or rebuilt.

item3.title: Flooring Throughout
item3.description: New flooring in all living spaces — hardwood, engineered wood, or LVP in main areas; tile in bathrooms and entryways.

item4.title: Paint & Trim
item4.description: Fresh paint on all walls and ceilings, new or refinished baseboards, door casings, and crown moulding where applicable.

item5.title: Electrical & Lighting
item5.description: Updated light fixtures, additional outlets where needed, dimmer switches, and panel upgrades for older homes.

item6.title: Doors & Hardware
item6.description: New interior doors, updated door handles and hinges, closet organizers, and any exterior door replacements as needed.

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips for Whole House Renovations

tip1.title: Prioritize and Phase Your Work
tip1.description: You don't have to do everything at once. Tackle the kitchen and bathrooms first (highest ROI), then address cosmetic updates in later phases. This spreads costs and lets you live in the home during renovations.

tip2.title: Keep Plumbing Where It Is
tip2.description: Relocating plumbing is one of the most expensive changes. Keeping kitchens and bathrooms in their current locations saves $5,000–$15,000 in plumbing and structural work.

tip3.title: Choose Materials Strategically
tip3.description: Splurge on high-impact, high-touch surfaces like kitchen countertops and bathroom tile. Save on areas like bedroom flooring (LVP looks great at half the cost of hardwood) and interior paint (mid-range paints perform nearly as well as premium).

tip4.title: Bundle for Better Pricing
tip4.description: Whole house renovations often cost less per room than doing rooms individually. Contractors offer better rates when they can schedule crews efficiently across a larger project.

faq.q1: How much does a whole house renovation cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Whole house renovation costs in Vancouver typically range from $23,000 to $150,000+ based on our completed projects. Cosmetic refreshes (paint, flooring, fixtures) start around $23,000–$35,000, comprehensive renovations with new kitchen and bathrooms run $40,000–$80,000, and premium transformations with structural changes can exceed $85,000.
faq.q2: How long does a whole house renovation take?
faq.a2: Most whole house renovations take 3 to 8 weeks depending on scope. A cosmetic refresh (paint, flooring, fixtures) can be done in 3–4 weeks. A comprehensive renovation including kitchen and bathrooms typically takes 5–8 weeks. Projects with structural changes or permits may take 10–14 weeks.
faq.q3: Can I live in my home during a whole house renovation?
faq.a3: It depends on the scope. For cosmetic refreshes, you can usually stay in the home as work is done room-by-room. For comprehensive renovations involving kitchen and bathroom demolition, we recommend arranging temporary accommodation for at least 2–4 weeks during the most disruptive phases.
faq.q4: What gives the best return on investment for a whole house renovation?
faq.a4: Kitchen and bathroom renovations consistently deliver the highest ROI in the Vancouver market. Updated flooring and fresh paint provide excellent value for relatively low cost. Open-concept layouts are popular with buyers but require structural work that increases costs significantly.
faq.q5: Do I need permits for a whole house renovation in Vancouver?
faq.a5: Yes, most whole house renovations require permits. Any work involving plumbing, electrical, gas, or structural changes needs a City of Vancouver building permit. Cosmetic-only work (paint, flooring, cabinets) typically does not require permits. Budget $500–$2,500 for permit fees.
